repo_prior_art.cite: none; API ExactID/PartialID catalogues are empty and cited q1/pid proposals are panel/network/dose-response, not LP/SVAR cumulants

published_prior_art.cite: PlagborgMollerWolf2021 + DrautzburgWright2023 imply common-restriction LP/VAR portability and independence-refined sign sets; the inclusion/equality statement is routine set algebra

repo_prior_art.cite: none; q1_spectral_phase_transition is Markov panel row-space rank, pid_network_exposure and pid_dose_response_iv are common-completion partial-ID motifs

published_prior_art.cite: DrautzburgWright2023 refines sign-restricted VARs through independence but does not give an LP/sign support-function equality/strictness boundary against cumulant-diagonalizing SVAR rotations

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue two-shock LP/SVAR non-Gaussian witness

published_prior_art.cite: LanneMeitzSaikkonen2017 and GourierouxMonfortRenne2017 give non-Gaussian SVAR/ICA identification, not a closed-form LP/sign versus finite diagonalizer support-gap certificate

soundness_flags.label: Assumption bounded-gap / R_SVAR definition
soundness_flags.one_line: The phrase 'any other cumulant equal to kappa_j is separated from it' is contradictory, and 'admissible kappa' in R_SVAR(P) is not pinned before it is used.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the bounded-gap condition with an explicit distinctness clause such as |kappa_l-kappa_j| >= delta_4 for l != j after sign/permutation normalization, and define the admissible kappa set in the formal setup.
Add one boundary corollary for equal kurtoses showing the diagonalizer is not unique and the proposed strict-gap statement intentionally excludes that case.

In Conjecture 2, spell out the promised two-shock trigonometric lower bound at least symbolically, including the amplitude and angular-distance terms, so the witness is auditable rather than only described.

recommended_next_step: Patch the cumulant-admissibility and delta_4 wording, then rerun novelty review; the kernel survives.
